questions.question: What is your average individual score?
How much silver do you own?
Describe your wiener using golf terms.
Why do you come on this trip?
When do you plan on arriving to the course? (We can check in as early as Monday night if you're interested in getting moved in early. Plan is for first round of golf to be Tuesday afternoon.)

answers_by_number.2: Strengths: driver, woods, wedges; Weaknesses: putter, consistency
answers_by_number.3: The only silver I own is in my beard and my pubes.
answers_by_number.4: I call my wiener Old Tom Morris.
answers_by_number.5: I come on this trip because it’s the most fun golf environment to play in, no question, along with being able to do it with lifelong friends. I’ve been on 20+ trips, and there’s never been a bad one.
answers_by_number.6: No specific plans yet, but I intend to be there to participate in the Tuesday round.

answers_by_number.2: Strengths are my ability to rape at any moment.  Weakness can be my ability to rape at any moment.
answers_by_number.3: I own two silver dollars. Or are they half dollars? No clue.
answers_by_number.4: My cock always ends up in the hole. #teamrape
answers_by_number.5: I come on this trip for questions like number 4. Also because the amount of fun had is unlike anything you can experience outside of this trip.
answers_by_number.6: Hoping I can hitch a ride again with someone Tuesday morning.

answers_by_number.2: Putting / Driving
answers_by_number.3: Clearly not enough
answers_by_number.4: Outdated but proven
answers_by_number.5: This trip is as good of a golf environment as Augusta
answers_by_number.6: Tuesday afternoon

answers_by_number.1: Average individual score for 18 is approximately 82.
answers_by_number.2: Strengths - I am fairly consistent and usually (but not always) don't blow up on holes, so I can keep my individual scores the same most of the time.  Biggest weakness is lack of distance (based on age), which makes me a bad A player as I can't come anywhere close to the BIG hitters.
answers_by_number.3: Not sure what silver we are referring to, but I don't invest in silver, gold or bitcoin.
answers_by_number.4: Short and hooking left.
answers_by_number.5: Enjoy the golf and the young (yes, except for Fulk, Max and Rob, I am the old guy) friends I have made.  I love people who just have fun golfing no matter how they play and this group is that.  Plus this is a very welcoming group, so even though I am a fairly new edition to Camp, I never felt like an outsider.
answers_by_number.6: My plan is to get their early Wednesday.  Not sure if I can make the warm up round on that day (will try), but will be there for the official Wednesday afternoon round, and will stay through the Saturday morning round.  I wish I could add on to the trip, but have two other golf trips this year.

answers_by_number.1: Average Individual score? 84-87
answers_by_number.2:
Strengths: Distance and Putting. If I can get the driver working, we’re going to make some cash. Eagles should be in play. I’m pretty good at reading greens and finding the line. I’m a great pair with someone who can chip and find the fairway off the tee, so I can send it. 
Weakness: Chipping and Consistency: Chipping is hard, but I’m working on it. I’m learning a new driver and irons, so consistency is a bit off.
answers_by_number.3: I own 69 x 0 troy oz of Ag
answers_by_number.4: It's like a short par 5. The ladies can't resist going for it, but after 8 strokes it's just embarrassing and they just pick up and go home
answers_by_number.5: This is year 2 for me. I stumbled into this trip because my screen name is RumpleSmoothSkin. Nick couldn’t resist. I still fell like a rookie and I’m humbled to be invited back and made to feel like a welcome part of Golf Camp. We all work so hard to get through life. Trips like Golf Camp make it all worth to be alive and inspire us to enjoy the time we have. Much Love
answers_by_number.6: I plan to arrive Monday night, I’m going to work a half day and then book it tot KY. Should be in 7-8pm. I’ll leave Saturday after the money round.

answers_by_number.2: Irons and chipping
answers_by_number.3: Not sure on the answer, but actually about $4k worth!
answers_by_number.4: Well my drive on a good day-like a missile and on a bad day- pulls to the right!
answers_by_number.5: Playing golf with this group is like no other golf experience! The good, the crazy, the funny, outrageous and a great escape!
answers_by_number.6: Wednesday afternoon round!

answers_by_number.1: Average score is 82 I’d say
answers_by_number.2: Strengths: wedge play/short game - I chip in once a round. Easier to do if you miss a lot of greens. Weakness: off the tee - this is where most of my penalties come
answers_by_number.3: I own 6 1oz American Eagle coins
answers_by_number.4: Reachable par 4 with a slightly elevated green
answers_by_number.5: I have never been before, but am very eager to start this journey of debauchery. I love the game, I love gambling, and I love bro-ing out. I’ve been told this is a great group of guys and I very much want to be a part of it.
answers_by_number.6: I will be arriving with Adam Tuesday morning

answers_by_number.5: I come on the trip because it’s a yearly tradition for me to spend quality time with a lot of guys I’ve made close friendships with, to relive old memories and to create new ones. It’s the best trip I take every year

answers_by_number.1: High 80s on a good day. Low 90s on a bad.
answers_by_number.2: I bring the weather! Birdie whistles and didgeridoos. Can be one of the longest off the tee, very solid short game. While I provide a lot of “A” shots, you’ll always get the D when paired with me.
answers_by_number.3: At least 30-40 nickels in my daughter’s crayola crayon piggy banks. Should I break into those yet?
answers_by_number.4: I don’t struggle with performance anxiety and can be long, when everything is lined up well. I don’t like playing in the cold. I also always play with only the best balls.
answers_by_number.5: Is this 20 years now? Craziness! I come for the stories. There is always something new, but the stories live forever.
answers_by_number.6: I will be there late Tuesday night at earliest, but it may be early Wednesday morning. Still TbD (first money round is still Wed afternoon, right?).

answers_by_number.4: It's my driver, but I've heard it referred to as a smooth iron. It’s got a head that demands respect, a shaft that does most of the work, and a tendency to get extra stiff when the pressure’s on. Starts the round tucked neatly in the bag, but give it the right conditions and suddenly it’s out on the fairway, standing at full attention like it’s about to tee off at Augusta.
